metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'enriched' in the five-specialist-overlays AC with a specific, observable minimum bar per overlay (e.g., 'each overlay incorporates at least the FP-guards and defect-class checks identified in the dso gap report, with a corresponding eval case seeded from a dso FP incident').
findings.finding: 'All five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) enriched' is effort-framed with no minimum observable bar — an executor cannot determine when enrichment is complete or sufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) enriched.'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the AC as: 'Each of the five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) is updated with (a) at least the FP-guards documented in the dso gap report for that domain, and (b) at least one new eval recall-case and one no-fire-case seeded from a dso-documented incident — verified by the eval scorer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard inline in the AC (e.g., 'half 1: existence of tests for the primary success path; half 2: existence of tests for failure/edge-case paths') or add a named-section pointer to the dso rubric document that defines them.
findings.finding: 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' uses 'both halves' as an undefined term — an executor reading only this plan cannot identify the two halves without consulting external documents not named in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the 'no kernel/verification-contract change shared with plan-review' constraint and the AC mandating a Pass-2 verifier prompt upgrade — either clarify that a prompt-content upgrade is NOT a 'kernel/contract change' (and define the boundary), or gate the Pass-2 upgrade behind a separate linked epic with its own plan-review coordination.
findings.finding: The Context constraint 'No kernel/verification-contract change shared with plan-review' appears to forbid changes to the generic Pass-2 verifier (which is shared), yet AC explicitly mandates 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP).' A prompt upgrade to a shared kernel component IS a kernel change, creating a direct cross-section contradiction.
findings.location: Context (last bullet) vs AC 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical clarification in Context: 'No kernel/verification-contract change shared with plan-review (prompt-content enrichment that preserves the existing verification contract interface is permitted).' Alternatively, add an explicit note in the AC that this upgrade is a content-only change and does not alter the verification-contract signature or shared type definitions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either define 'story A' explicitly within this plan (e.g., as the first implementation story in a decomposition section) or replace the forward reference with a self-contained description of who records the baseline and when.
findings.finding: 'story A' is referenced in Success Criteria as the named vehicle for recording the pre-change baseline ('story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run…'), but the plan contains no story decomposition, no definition of 'story A,' and no described work that implements it. The AC 'A pre-change baseline is recorded' covers the outcome but not the named entity, leaving the reference an orphan.
findings.location: Success Criteria, sentence beginning 'story A records the current…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'trailer-referenced tickets' in the plan body (e.g., 'Git commit trailer lines such as Fixes: #123 or Closes: #456') or add a reference to the convention document that specifies the format.
findings.finding: 'trailer-referenced tickets' is a key concept in AC 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets' but is undefined anywhere in the plan body. No description explains what constitutes a trailer, how tickets are extracted from it, or what format is expected.
findings.location: AC: 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan body: 'Trailer-referenced tickets: issue/ticket identifiers extracted from standard Git commit trailer lines (e.g., Fixes: #N, Closes: #N, Relates-to: #N) present in the PR's commit messages or description.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inline the exact text of the 'positive linter-deference line' into the AC (or cite the document/section where it is defined), and add a pointer to the experiment or eval result that backs it.
findings.finding: The AC 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only' references a specific piece of content ('experiment-backed positive linter-deference line') and implies prior experimental evidence, but neither the line's content, the experiment, nor its results are described or cited anywhere in the plan body.
findings.location: AC: 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan for post-merge regressions: specify the rollback trigger (e.g., metric threshold breach on live corpus), the revert mechanism (e.g., git revert + re-run baseline eval), and who owns the decision — eval gating alone is a forward guard, not a rollback path.
findings.finding: The plan changes the base prompt, Pass-2 verifier prompt, all five specialist overlays, and adds a new conflict-marker detector — all of which alter behavior that existing gate consumers rely on — but states no explicit back-out/rollback plan if a change passes eval yet regresses in production or on a corpus not covered by the eval suite.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria (overall plan)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Success Criteria or a dedicated Rollback section, add: 'If post-merge nit-rate or FP-verify-drop rate exceeds baseline by >X% on the live eval corpus within N days, the change is reverted via git revert <sha> and the eval seeds are augmented with the regressing cases before re-landing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or task that captures the content-triggered overlay pattern, FP-ledger convention, and telemetry stage design in a durable artifact (CLAUDE.md update, ADR, or design doc) before the epic closes.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple novel architectural decisions — content-triggered overlays as a new pattern, an FP-ledger convention, and grounding-effort/health telemetry as a new pipeline stage — without specifying that any of these decisions will be captured in a durable artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design doc).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — lines: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped', 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '[ ] A CLAUDE.md or ADR entry documents (a) the content-triggered overlay contract and OVERLAY_IDS registration convention, (b) the FP-ledger schema and update workflow, and (c) the grounding-effort/health telemetry data model and consumer interface.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decompose the omnibus 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note' AC into separate lines each naming a specific artifact (file, schema, prompt section, or doc entry) that a reviewer can inspect.
findings.finding: The AC line 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed' uses 'landed' without specifying an end-state-observable proof — no artifact type, location, or acceptance test is named.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism by which the scope-intent overlay receives referenced-ticket content: detail how git commit trailers are parsed, which step fetches the union of referenced tickets, and how that content is threaded into the overlay's context (e.g., as a new assembly step that populates a `referenced_tickets` context field alongside the diff).
findings.finding: The scope-intent overlay AC requires it to 'check the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets,' but the existing CodeReviewBatchRunner injects only diff context into each overlay (no ticket-reading capability per overlay), and the plan specifies no mechanism for parsing git commit trailers, fetching the referenced tickets, or threading that data into the overlay's context — leaving the AC's most distinctive behaviour with an architectural gap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `assemble_trailer_tickets` step between `assemble_diff` and the base reviewer that: (1) runs `git log -1 --format=%B` to extract the commit message, (2) parses `Fixes:`, `Closes:`, `Ref:` etc. trailers to collect ticket ids, (3) calls `rebar.show_ticket` for each, and (4) emits `referenced_ticket_contexts` as a new workflow input threaded into the scope-intent overlay's StepContext — the same pattern the plan_review gate uses for ticket context assembly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'content-triggered' means a new deterministic pre-scan modality (e.g., diff-line-count heuristic) added to overlay_union's formula, or simply model-driven escalation via recommend_overlays — and if the former, state why the base reviewer's existing recommend signal is insufficient for deletion-impact detection.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that 'content-triggered overlay capability' is distinct from glob-triggered and base-escalation overlays and that 'deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it,' but provides no positive rationale for why content-triggering is a new first-class modality rather than model-driven escalation via the base reviewer's existing recommend_overlays signal (which can already detect 'this diff has large deletions' from the diff context).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'content-triggered' overlays are Round-A deterministic triggers or Round-B agent-escalations, explain why the existing applies_to glob axis is insufficient for deletion-impact/scope-intent detection, and document how the new trigger axis respects the ADR 0010 one-hop/cap invariants (or update that ADR).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'content-triggered overlay capability' (AC#4) — overlays triggered by diff content rather than file-path globs — as a new id registered in OVERLAY_IDS, but the existing overlay escalation design (ADR 0010) only recognises two axes: Round-A glob triggers (applies_to globs in criteria_routing.json) and Round-B agent escalation (recommend_overlays). Introducing a third, content-based trigger axis is a novel architectural addition to that design, yet the plan does not compare it to the established ADR 0010 pattern, explain why the existing glob/agent-escalation axes are insufficient, or justify why this new axis does not require an ADR update.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#4: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve whether 'prefer AST-LSP' in the verifier prompt means (a) a soft instruction to an already-tool-enabled verifier, (b) a new tool-injection plug-point added to the kernel consumer seam, or (c) a prompt-only heuristic — and if (b), reconcile with the plan's stated constraint that no kernel/verification-contract change is shared with plan-review.
findings.finding: AC#6 specifies upgrading the Pass-2 verifier prompt to 'prefer AST-LSP' — an assertion that the verifier has or will gain access to AST/LSP tools. The current code-review verifier (code-review-verify.md) re-grounds findings against a DIFF passed as a context string via verify_findings(findings, *, context, ...); the kernel's verify pass is a prompt + structured-output call with no injected tools. The plan does not verify or explain how AST/LSP tool access would be wired into the kernel's verify pass for code-review without changing the kernel consumer seam.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#6: 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval_solver code-review arm's execution model (inline overlay-finder vs. full four-pass workflow) and its fixture schema (diff_text/changed_files fields) before committing to AC ordering, to confirm the FakeRunner + MemoryRecorder seam is sufficient or identify what must be added.
findings.finding: The plan asserts an `eval_solver` code-review arm (AC#1) as a straightforward addition, but `eval_solver.run_case` currently has no code-review dispatch path. The existing agentic solver arms (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) stand up a lightweight ticket/epic store; a code-review arm must instead materialise a git diff context and drive the full four-pass `gates/code-review.yaml` workflow (including a real or synthetic diff, the overlay-union logic, the two-round escalation, Pass-2 verify, and Pass-3 decide). The plan does not specify whether the arm runs individual overlay finders inline (like the plan-review criterion arm) or the full four-pass workflow — a design gap that determines both the eval fixture schema and whether the existing `FakeRunner` + `MemoryRecorder` seam is sufficient.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#1: 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sub-task to AC#8 for authoring the conflict-marker detector implementation (regex scan over diff text for <<<<<<</=======/>>>>>>> markers), separate from the routing-entry wiring, so both halves are explicitly scoped.
findings.finding: AC#8 states 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion,' treating the wiring as the only task. However, no conflict-marker detector exists in the grounding detector registry (`src/rebar/grounding/detectors/`), `engine_b`, or `detectors.py`. A DET criterion requires a backing detector implementation before it can be registered in `criteria_routing.json` and dispatched by `run_detectors`. The plan silently conflates authoring the detector with wiring it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#8: 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion.'
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC#8 into two steps: (a) implement a `rebar.builtin.conflicts.conflict-markers` detector in the grounding registry (a pure regex scan over the diff, no external tool needed); (b) register it as `exec: DET, fail_mode: open` in `criteria_routing.json` with `detector: {id: 'rebar.builtin.conflicts.conflict-markers'}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact canonical string tokens for the two new overlay ids ('deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent') and their insertion position in the OVERLAY_IDS tuple, so implementers, drift-guard tests, and workflow flag derivation are unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan introduces two new overlay ids ('deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent') that must be registered in the closed OVERLAY_IDS enum, but neither the id tokens nor their position in the ordered tuple are defined anywhere in the plan. OVERLAY_IDS (registry.py:34-46, confirmed) is the single source of truth for prompt dispatch, workflow YAML `when` flags, drift-guard tests (test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:148-155), and move catalog applies_when validation — an executor cannot wire these overlays without knowing their canonical string ids.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan section or story note: 'New OVERLAY_IDS entries: "deletion-impact" (after "db-migrations") and "scope-intent" (after "llm-prompts"); the drift-guard test assertion in test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py must be updated to include both.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the conflict-marker DET criterion's full routing entry: criterion id string, detector id or id_prefix, applies_to globs (if any), fail_mode ('open'), block_threshold, and blocking_enabled (false), so the entry can be added to criteria_routing.json and wired through the DET consumer.
findings.finding: The plan's AC requires a 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion,' but provides no criterion id string, no detector selector (id or id_prefix), no applies_to globs, and no fail_mode value. The data-driven DET consumer (detectors.py + registry.det_criteria()) reads ALL of these fields from criteria_routing.json — the routing entry needed for the conflict-marker criterion is completely unspecified in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the code-review eval case input schema (required fields: e.g. diff_text, changed_files, expected_fire boolean) and the gating scorer contract (what output field(s) determine pass/fail), so eval fixture authors and scorer implementers have an unambiguous specification.
findings.finding: AC requires 'an eval_solver code-review arm' but the plan does not define the case input schema for a code-review eval case — specifically: which fields a case dict carries (diff_text? changed_files? ticket_id? base/head?), what the expected output shape is, and how the gating scorer for 'fire' vs 'no-fire' is defined. eval_solver.run_case (confirmed in eval_solver.py:106-171) currently has no code-review dispatch branch; the three existing agentic ops each have a precisely specified case schema (ticket_context, ticket_type, epics, spec fields).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'two halves' of the behavioral-testing standard in the plan (e.g. quote or summarize the dso rubric halves) so the tests overlay AC is checkable by inspection.
findings.finding: AC says 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' — but the plan never defines what the 'two halves' of the behavioral-testing standard are. The current code-review-tests.md (confirmed read) has a single flat section with no halves. 'Both halves' is an opaque reference to a dso concept that is not unpacked anywhere in the plan, making the AC unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the protocol for delivering multi-ticket context to the scope-intent overlay: specify the workflow step that fetches and concatenates trailer-referenced ticket descriptions, the prompt variable it injects (e.g. {{referenced_tickets}}), and how the overlay prompt uses it.
findings.finding: AC requires the scope-intent overlay to 'check the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets,' but no processing protocol is defined for how the overlay prompt receives multi-ticket context. All existing overlay prompts receive only {{ticket_context}} (a single assembled diff context, confirmed in code-review-security.md and code-review-tests.md). No mechanism — fetch step, prompt variable, context assembly op — is described for constructing or injecting the multi-ticket UNION into the scope-intent overlay's prompt.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] f93a must add an explicit AC line: record pre-change gate metrics (findings/run, nit-rate, FP/verify-drop rate) over the eval corpus as a named baseline artifact before any other story lands.
findings.finding: Parent Success Criteria explicitly require 'story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus as the baseline,' and Parent AC #2 states 'A pre-change baseline is recorded so the measurable-improvement criterion is checkable.' f93a's AC covers only the nit-framing experiment as a committed eval case baseline, not a full gate metrics snapshot (findings/run, nit-rate, FP/verify-drop rate). No other sibling claims this responsibility.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Add to f93a's Acceptance Criteria: '[ ] Pre-change gate baseline recorded: findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate measured over the eval corpus and committed as a baseline artifact (e.g., eval_specs/baseline_metrics.json) before any prompt/detector change lands.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify ownership split: f93a authors the harness skeleton and minimal seed specs; da8a owns the definitive tests+security overlay eval specs — document this handoff explicitly in both stories.
findings.finding: f93a claims ownership of eval specs for 'at least the tests + security overlays' while da8a-75f5-ccb2-4b5f also claims 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for all six overlays (including tests and security). The authoritative owner of tests and security overlay eval specs is ambiguous, risking duplication, conflicting fixture assumptions, or a gap if each story assumes the other authored those specs.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Add to f93a's scope: 'seed tests+security eval spec stubs (fixture shape only, no content assertions) to prove the harness wiring; da8a replaces stubs with full recall+no-fire assertions when overlay content lands.' Add to da8a: 'takes ownership of definitive tests+security eval specs; f93a stubs are superseded.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Both f983 and 3c5d must declare registry.py as a shared file with explicit coordination / serialization (same mechanism as criteria_routing.json).
findings.finding: Both f983-9441-35f4-445f (adds `deletion-impact` to OVERLAY_IDS) and 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a (adds `scope-intent` to OVERLAY_IDS) edit registry.py, but neither story explicitly coordinates with the other on registry.py as a shared file. f983's coordination notes name criteria_routing.json and da25/3c5d for that file, but registry.py coordination with 3c5d is absent.
findings.suggested_fix: In both f983 and 3c5d, add registry.py to the file_impact / coordination note alongside criteria_routing.json, and ensure next_batch serializes them (or one story explicitly depends on the other's registry.py change).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] f93a must clarify: the VCS conflict-marker recall case is either (a) a stub/placeholder that becomes gating only after da25 lands, or (b) da25 owns that recall case's spec and f93a only seeds the harness infrastructure for it.
findings.finding: f93a seeds a 'VCS conflict marker' seeded-defect recall case as part of the eval spec corpus, but da25-8af4-7c15-4b4e is the story that ships the conflict-marker detector. If f93a lands first (as the gating foundation), the conflict-marker recall case targets a detector that does not yet exist — the case will vacuously fail or be skipped. The dependency order between f93a's recall spec and da25's detector is not called out.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Move ownership of the VCS conflict-marker recall eval spec to da25 (which already has a 'Recall+no-fire eval passes' AC). f93a's seeded-defect list should note 'conflict-marker recall spec owned by da25' to avoid duplication.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
